name: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden
notes: Beautiful garden with cherry blossoms in spring. Entry fee required.

name: Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building (Observation Deck)
notes: Free observation deck with panoramic Tokyo views including Mt. Fuji on clear days.

name: Kabukicho (Entertainment District)
notes: Tokyo's busiest entertainment district. Safe to walk at night.

name: Golden Gai
notes: Labyrinth of tiny atmospheric bars. Unique Tokyo experience.

name: Shibuya Crossing
notes: World's busiest scramble crossing. Best viewed from Starbucks or Mag's Park above.

name: Meiji Jingu Shrine
notes: Forested Shinto shrine dedicated to Emperor Meiji. Peaceful contrast to Harajuku crowds.

name: Takeshita Street (Harajuku)
notes: Youth fashion and street food. Crepes, quirky shops, Harajuku culture.

name: Omotesando Hills
notes: Upscale spiral shopping complex designed by Tadao Ando.

name: Senso-ji Temple
notes: Tokyo's oldest and most famous temple. Especially beautiful at night.

name: Nakamise Shopping Street
notes: 250-meter traditional souvenir shopping street leading to Senso-ji.

name: Sumida River Cruise
notes: Scenic river cruise from Asakusa toward Odaiba. Great city views.

name: Tokyo Skytree
notes: World's second tallest structure. Two observation decks.

name: Tsukiji Outer Market
notes: Famous fish and food market. Great for fresh sushi breakfast.

name: Ginza Six
notes: Flagship luxury shopping center with rooftop garden.

name: teamLab Planets Tokyo
notes: Popular immersive digital art installation. Book tickets in advance.

name: Akihabara Electric Town
notes: World center for electronics, anime, manga, and gaming culture.

name: Yodobashi Akiba
notes: Massive electronics and gadget store. Tax-free for tourists.

name: Kanda Myojin Shrine
notes: Historic shrine popular with tech workers and gamers.

name: Sunshine City Aquarium
notes: Popular aquarium inside the Sunshine City complex.

name: Ikebukuro Animate
notes: Japan's largest anime merchandise store. 9 floors.

name: Rikkyo University Campus
notes: Beautiful red-brick Gothic Revival campus. Free to walk around.

name: Dotonbori Canal Walk
notes: Iconic neon-lit canal. Best at night. Famous Glico Running Man sign.

name: Shinsaibashi Shopping Arcade
notes: Covered shopping street, one of Japan's oldest. Mix of brands and souvenirs.

name: Hozenji Yokocho
notes: Moss-covered stone lanterns, tiny Fuudo-san temple. Atmospheric alley.

name: Shinsekai District
notes: 1920s retro district with Tsutenkaku Tower. Great for kushikatsu and atmosphere.

name: Osaka Umeda Sky Building
notes: Futuristic floating garden observatory. Stunning Osaka city views.

name: HEP Five Ferris Wheel
notes: Red ferris wheel on top of a shopping mall. Unique Umeda photo spot.

name: Amerika-mura (American Village)
notes: Youth fashion hub with vintage clothes, street art, and pop culture.

name: Osaka Castle
notes: Osaka's iconic landmark. Grounds are beautiful year-round. Museum inside.

name: Fushimi Inari Taisha
notes: Thousands of torii gates on a mountain trail. Go early to avoid crowds.

name: Kiyomizudera Temple
notes: Iconic wooden stage temple on a hill. Panoramic Kyoto views.

name: Hanamikoji Street (Gion)
notes: Preserved geisha district street. Best at dusk for maiko sightings.

name: Yasaka Shrine
notes: Free entry, 24/7 access. Beautiful lanterns at night.

name: Nijo Castle
notes: UNESCO World Heritage site with nightingale floors and ornate interiors.

name: Kyoto Tower Observation Deck
notes: Best views of Kyoto from above the station. Good at night.

name: Tofuku-ji Temple
notes: Famous Zen garden. Peak autumn foliage spot in late November.

name: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove
notes: Iconic tall bamboo forest path. Go before 8am to avoid crowds.

name: Tenryu-ji Temple Garden
notes: UNESCO World Heritage garden with pond and mountain backdrop.

name: Togetsukyo Bridge
notes: Arashiyama's iconic bridge over the Oi River. Best in cherry blossom and autumn seasons.

name: Okochi Sanso Villa
notes: Former samurai actor's villa with multiple tea houses and gardens.

name: Todai-ji Temple (Great Buddha)
notes: UNESCO World Heritage. Home to Japan's largest bronze Buddha. Deer roam freely outside.

name: Nara Deer Park
notes: Over 1,000 free-roaming deer considered sacred messengers. Feed with shika senbei.

name: Kasuga Taisha Shrine
notes: UNESCO shrine famous for thousands of stone and bronze lanterns.

name: Yoshikien Garden
notes: Serene traditional garden. Free entry for foreign visitors.

name: Hiroshima Peace Memorial Museum
notes: Essential visit. Powerful and moving A-bomb memorial museum.

name: Atomic Bomb Dome (Genbaku Dome)
notes: Preserved ruins of the industrial building directly below the bomb. Free, open 24/7.

name: Peace Memorial Park
notes: Beautiful riverside park with multiple memorials. Center of Hiroshima's peace tourism.

name: Hiroshima Castle
notes: Reconstructed castle with museum. Nice moat and grounds.

name: Itsukushima Shrine
notes: Famous floating torii gate. UNESCO World Heritage. Best at high tide.

name: Mt. Misen Hike
notes: Hike or ropeway to Miyajima's highest peak. Panoramic Inland Sea views.

name: Miyajima Ferry
notes: Short ferry crossing from Miyajimaguchi. Covered by JR Pass. Great views of the torii.
